name: savory pie crust
instructions: 1 cup all-purpose flour, 1/2 cup wheat flour, 1 tbsp sugar, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/2 cup fat split between butter and vegetable shortening (or lard — see notes), more butter than the second fat. Bake at 400°F.
notes:
Some recipes call for a much higher flour-to-fat ratio but this works well enough for a brittle/flaky crust. Shortening makes the crust easier to form and stretchier; butter makes it flaky/brittle. More butter than the second fat is ideal.

LARD INSTEAD OF SHORTENING: lard can substitute for the vegetable shortening 1:1 and gives a noticeably better flavor — savory, slightly meaty depth that vegetable shortening can't deliver. Use lard when the filling can stand a slightly less-neutral crust (most savory pie fillings, anything mushroom or meat). Stick with shortening when the filling wants a fully neutral crust.

PAN SIZE: this recipe fits an 11-inch pie tray exactly. Scale up or down accordingly for other sizes.
